Classification of the vehicle unit by its condition and ownership history: 'new' = new vehicle never titled; 'used' = previously owned/titled vehicle; 'certified_pre_owned' = OEM-certified used vehicle meeting specific age/mileage criteria; 'demo' = dealer demonstration vehicle; 'loaner' = service loaner vehicle. Governs applicable pricing, warranty, and financing programs.

The OEM invoice price charged to the dealer for this vehicle unit, representing the dealer's acquisition cost from the manufacturer before holdback, dealer cash, or other incentives. Confidential commercial pricing data.
The date on which the asking price for this vehicle unit was most recently updated in the CDK Global DMS. Used to track pricing strategy cadence and ensure market-aligned pricing for aged inventory.

The physical lot or storage location code within the dealership where this vehicle is parked (e.g., 'LOT-A-12', 'SHOWROOM-3', 'OVERFLOW-B'). Used for lot management, vehicle locator, and PDI workflow routing within CDK Global DMS.

The Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for this specific vehicle unit including all factory-installed options and packages, expressed in the local currency (USD). Established by the OEM and used as the baseline for dealer pricing and incentive calculations.

Current odometer reading of the vehicle in miles at the time of inventory check-in or last update. Critical for used, demo, and loaner vehicles to determine CPO eligibility, warranty coverage, and pricing. For new vehicles, reflects delivery mileage.

Indicates whether the Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) has been completed for this vehicle unit. PDI is a mandatory dealer quality check performed before vehicle delivery to a customer, verifying all systems, fluids, and accessories meet OEM standards. True = PDI completed; False = PDI pending.
The date on which the Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) was completed and signed off by the dealer's service technician. Null if PDI has not yet been performed.
The NHTSA-assigned recall campaign number if this vehicle is subject to an active safety recall (e.g., '23V-123'). Null if no active recall. Used for recall remedy tracking and regulatory reporting.

Indicates whether this vehicle unit is subject to an active NHTSA safety recall that prevents retail sale or delivery until the recall remedy has been performed. The date the vehicle unit was physically received and checked into the dealer's inventory from the OEM transport carrier or trade-in. Marks the start of the days-on-lot aging clock and floor plan financing period.
The timestamp when this dealer inventory record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.

The timestamp when this dealer inventory record was most recently updated in the Silver Layer lakehouse, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, incremental processing, and audit trail requirements.

Indicates how the dealer acquired this vehicle unit: 'factory_order' = ordered directly from OEM; 'dealer_trade' = acquired via dealer-to-dealer trade; 'auction' = purchased at wholesale auction; 'trade_in' = accepted as customer trade-in; 'fleet_return' = returned from fleet/rental program; 'lease_return' = returned at end of lease term.
Dealer-assigned internal stock number used within the CDK Global DMS to identify and locate this vehicle on the lot. Unique within a dealership but not globally unique.

Type of transmission fitted to the vehicle: automatic, manual, CVT (Continuously Variable Transmission), DCT (Dual-Clutch Transmission), or single_speed (for BEV). Used for inventory filtering and customer preference matching.

Current transportation status for vehicles in the delivery pipeline from OEM plant to dealership: 'not_shipped' = awaiting dispatch from plant; 'in_transit' = en route via carrier; 'delivered' = arrived at dealer; 'rail' = currently on rail transport; 'truck' = currently on truck transport.

URL link to the digital Monroney window sticker (required by the Automobile Information Disclosure Act) for this vehicle unit, hosted by the OEM or a third-party service. Contains MSRP, standard equipment, options, fuel economy ratings, and safety information.

business_glossary_term: DMS (Dealer Management System) Integration Log ID
description: Unique identifier for each DMS integration event log entry. Primary key for the integration log table.
Identifier of the dealership for which this integration event occurred. Links to the dealer master data.
Identifier of the dealership for which this integration event occurred. Links to the dealer master data.
Authentication mechanism used to verify dealer DMS identity and authorize data access during synchronization.
Assessment of business impact severity if this sync event failed. Critical failures affect customer-facing operations; low impact affects reporting only.
Indicates whether data compression was enabled for this synchronization event to optimize bandwidth usage.
Date and time when this integration log record was first created in the OEM data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Time difference in minutes between when data was last updated in the OEM system and when it was synchronized to the dealer DMS. Key metric for monitoring data currency.
OEM-assigned unique code identifying the dealership. Used for business reporting and dealer network management.
Legal business name of the dealership. Denormalized for reporting convenience and to support historical tracking if dealer names change.
Type of delivery-related event logged from DMS.
The specific CDK Global DMS API endpoint URL used for this synchronization event. Identifies the dealer system instance and integration point.
Unique transaction identifier generated by the CDK Global DMS for this sync event. Used for end-to-end traceability and support escalation.
Version number of the CDK Global DMS software running at the dealership. Used to track compatibility and feature availability.
Security protocol used to encrypt data during transmission between OEM platform and dealer DMS. Ensures data confidentiality and integrity.
Classification of the error type encountered during synchronization. Used for root cause analysis and troubleshooting dealer connectivity issues.
Detailed error message or description returned by the DMS or integration middleware. Provides technical details for troubleshooting failed sync events.
Indicates whether this failed sync event requires escalation to dealer support or OEM integration team for manual intervention.
Date and time when this sync failure was escalated to support teams. Null if no escalation occurred or sync succeeded.
Unique batch identifier assigned to group related synchronization events. Used to correlate multiple sync operations within a single integration cycle.
Date and time when this integration log record was last updated. Tracks changes to sync status, retry attempts, or resolution information.
Maximum number of retry attempts configured for this sync event type before escalation or manual intervention is required.
Scheduled date and time for the next automatic retry attempt if the current sync failed. Null if no retry is scheduled or sync succeeded.
Unique transaction identifier generated by the OEM integration platform for this sync event. Used for internal traceability and audit.
Size of the data payload transmitted during this sync event measured in kilobytes. Used to monitor bandwidth utilization and identify large transfers.
Number of records that failed to synchronize due to validation errors, connectivity issues, or data quality problems.
Total number of records successfully received and acknowledged by the dealer DMS. Used to validate completeness of data transfer.
Total number of records transmitted from the OEM platform to the dealer DMS during this sync event. Applicable for push operations.
Geographic region code where the dealership is located. Used for regional performance analysis and connectivity monitoring.
Free-text notes documenting the root cause and resolution steps taken for failed sync events. Used for knowledge management and trend analysis.
Date and time when a failed sync event was resolved and data synchronization completed successfully. Used to calculate mean time to resolution.
Sequential number indicating which retry attempt this represents for a failed synchronization. Zero indicates the initial attempt.
Indicates whether this synchronization event met the defined SLA target for duration and success rate. Used for dealer performance scorecards.

business_glossary_term: SLA (Service Level Agreement) Target Duration in Seconds
description: Target synchronization duration in seconds as defined in the dealer integration SLA. Used to measure performance against contractual commitments.
Total elapsed time in seconds from sync initiation to completion or failure. Used to monitor DMS performance and identify slow connections.
Type of data synchronization event between OEM platform and CDK Global DMS. Indicates the direction and nature of data flow (push from OEM or pull from dealer).
Current status of the synchronization event indicating whether data transfer completed successfully, partially succeeded, or failed.
Date and time when the synchronization event was initiated. This is the principal business event timestamp for the integration log entry.
Unique identifier for the floor plan financing record.
FK to the F&I contract for dealer floor plan financing.
Reference to the dealer holding this floor plan financing arrangement.
Reference to the dealer holding this floor plan financing arrangement.
Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Floor‑plan financing is calculated per SKU to reflect cost, interest, and regulatory limits.
Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Floor plan financing is per‑vehicle; linking to VIN registry enables accurate interest calculation and balance tracking.
Unique account number assigned by the financing institution to the dealer's floor plan credit facility.
Boolean indicator flagging vehicles that have exceeded the aging threshold defined in the floor plan agreement (typically 90, 120, or 180 days). True indicates aged inventory requiring attention.
Number of days after which inventory is considered aged per the floor plan agreement terms. Triggers curtailment requirements or OEM assistance programs.

Result status of the most recent floor plan audit. Passed indicates compliance; failed indicates discrepancies; pending indicates audit in progress; not_required indicates no audit needed; exception indicates special circumstances.
Timestamp when this floor plan financing record was first created in the system.
Total credit line amount approved by the financing institution for the dealer's floor plan facility. Represents the maximum borrowing capacity.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this floor plan record (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD).
Required curtailment payment amount for aged inventory, typically a percentage of the original financed amount after a specified aging period (e.g., 90 or 120 days).
Scheduled date by which the dealer must make a curtailment payment (partial paydown) on aged inventory per the floor plan agreement terms.
Boolean indicator of whether the required curtailment payment has been made by the dealer. True indicates paid; False indicates outstanding.
Daily interest accrual amount calculated based on the outstanding balance and interest rate. Used for dealer financial health monitoring.
Number of days the vehicle has been in dealer inventory under floor plan financing, calculated from financing start date to current date or sale date.
Original dealer invoice amount for the vehicle, typically the basis for the floor plan financed amount.
Date when the dealer was declared in default on floor plan obligations for this vehicle. Null if no default has occurred.
Boolean indicator of whether the dealer has defaulted on floor plan payments for this vehicle. True indicates default; False indicates current status.

business_glossary_term: Dealer Management System (DMS) Floor Plan ID
description: External identifier for this floor plan record in the dealer's DMS (e.g., CDK Global). Used for system integration and reconciliation.
Date when floor plan financing for this vehicle unit was terminated, either through retail sale and payoff or other resolution.
Internal or industry-standard code identifying the financing institution for system integration and reporting.
Name of the financial institution or captive finance company providing the floor plan credit line (e.g., OEM captive finance, third-party lender).
Date when floor plan financing for this vehicle unit commenced. Typically the date the vehicle was received into dealer inventory and financed.

Current lifecycle status of the floor plan financing record. Active indicates ongoing financing; paid_off indicates vehicle sold and financing settled; defaulted indicates payment failure; suspended indicates temporary hold; pending_audit indicates under review; curtailed indicates partial paydown required.
Annual interest rate percentage charged by the financing institution on the outstanding floor plan balance for this vehicle.
Date of the most recent floor plan audit conducted by the financing institution or OEM to verify physical inventory and compliance with financing terms.
Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price for the financed vehicle. Used for margin analysis and dealer financial health assessment.
Scheduled date for the next floor plan audit. Used for audit planning and dealer notification.
Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special circumstances related to this floor plan financing record (e.g., audit exceptions, payment arrangements, OEM approvals).
Dollar amount of OEM assistance provided for this floor plan record, such as interest rate buy-down or direct payment subsidy.

business_glossary_term: Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) Assistance Program Code
description: Code identifying any OEM floor plan assistance program applied to this vehicle (e.g., interest subsidy, extended aging allowance, special financing terms for slow-moving models).
Current outstanding principal balance owed to the financing institution for this specific vehicle unit. Updated upon curtailment payments and final payoff.
Total amount paid to the financing institution to settle the floor plan obligation for this vehicle, including principal and accrued interest.
Date when the floor plan financing for this vehicle was fully paid off, typically upon retail sale or dealer buyout.
The financed amount for this individual vehicle unit, typically equal to dealer invoice or a percentage thereof. Basis for interest calculation.
SAP financial document number associated with the floor plan financing transaction in the OEM's ERP system (SAP S/4HANA FI module).
Cumulative interest amount paid to the financing institution over the life of the floor plan financing for this vehicle unit.
Timestamp when this floor plan financing record was last modified. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Daily cost for yard storage under the floor plan.
Unique surrogate identifier for each dealer order record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the dealer_order data product.
Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Order‑to‑OTA Mapping links each vehicle order to its connected vehicle record for OTA update eligibility tracking.
Reference to the retail customer for whom this order is placed. Populated for retail (customer-specific) orders; null for stock orders.
Reference to the dealership that placed this order. Links to the dealer master record in the dealer domain.
Reference to the dealership that placed this order. Links to the dealer master record in the dealer domain.
Reference to the retail customer for whom this order is placed. Populated for retail (customer-specific) orders; null for stock orders.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Required for Dealer Order Fulfillment report linking each dealer order to the specific production order that will build the vehicle.
Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Dealer order processing and financing depend on the exact SKU to calculate MSRP, incentives, and regulatory compliance.
Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Dealer order reconciliation report matches each dealer purchase order to the OEM vehicle order that fulfills it.
Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Dealer order processing must associate each order with the specific VIN to enable production tracking and post‑sale service.
The actual date the vehicle was physically delivered to the dealership. Used to calculate On-Time Delivery (OTD) performance against the confirmed delivery date.
The actual date the vehicle departed the production plant or port for transit to the dealership. Used to measure logistics performance and update OTD tracking.
Code identifying the vehicle allocation pool or program from which this order is fulfilled (e.g., regional allocation, fleet pool, dealer stock pool). Governs allocation rules and constraints.

The body style classification of the ordered vehicle. Used for production scheduling, logistics planning, and sales mix reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sedan|coupe|SUV|truck|van|wagon|convertible — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Code identifying the reason for order cancellation (e.g., customer withdrawal, dealer over-allocation, duplicate order). Populated only when order_status is cancelled.
The OEM-confirmed delivery date communicated back to the dealer after production scheduling. May differ from the requested delivery date.
The timestamp when the dealer order record was first created in the system. Audit trail field conforming to IATF 16949 data traceability requirements.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this order (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Enables multi-currency reporting across dealer networks in different markets.

Number of calendar days the actual delivery date exceeded the confirmed delivery date. Zero or negative values indicate on-time or early delivery. Used for OTD variance analysis and dealer penalty calculations.
The wholesale invoice price charged by the OEM to the dealer for this vehicle order. Represents the net amount the dealer pays before any holdback or incentive adjustments.
Order reference number assigned by the CDK Global Dealer Management System at the dealership level. Enables reconciliation between OEM and dealer systems.
The estimated date the vehicle is expected to depart the production plant or port for transit to the dealership. Used for logistics planning and dealer communication.
OEM-assigned paint color code for the exterior of the ordered vehicle. Used for production scheduling, paint shop sequencing, and inventory matching.
Generic field service info placeholder
Code indicating the reason an order has been placed on hold, such as parts shortage, regulatory hold, credit hold, or customer change request. Null when order is not on hold.
Whether the customer requested home delivery for this order.

The regulatory homologation variant code specifying market-specific compliance configurations (e.g., US-spec, EU-spec, CARB-spec). Ensures the vehicle meets applicable NHTSA, EPA, UNECE, or CARB standards for the destination market.
Total OEM incentive or discount amount applied to this order, including dealer cash, consumer cash, and program incentives. Reduces the effective dealer cost.
OEM-assigned interior trim and color code for the ordered vehicle. Used for production configuration and dealer inventory matching.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the market where the vehicle will be sold. Determines applicable regulatory standards (NHTSA, EPA, CARB, Euro NCAP, UNECE), homologation requirements, and pricing.
The Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for the fully configured vehicle as ordered, in the base currency. Represents the gross base amount before dealer adjustments, incentives, or taxes.

Free-text notes or special instructions associated with the order, entered by the dealer or OEM order management team. May include customer preferences, special handling requirements, or escalation notes.
The calendar date on which the dealer submitted the order to the OEM. This is the principal business event date for the order transaction.

Current lifecycle state of the dealer order tracking progression from submission through delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|confirmed|scheduled|produced|shipped|delivered|cancelled — promote to reference product]
Classification of the order indicating whether it is a dealer stock replenishment order, a retail customer-specific order, a demonstration vehicle order, a fleet order, or a courtesy vehicle order.

Indicates whether the vehicle was delivered on or before the confirmed delivery date. True = delivered on time; False = delivered late. Core metric for OTD performance reporting and dealer satisfaction.
The date on which the Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) was completed at the dealership. Null if PDI has not yet been performed.
Indicates whether a Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) is required before the vehicle is handed over to the customer. True = PDI mandatory; False = PDI not required.
Priority classification assigned to the order for production scheduling and allocation purposes. Higher priority orders are scheduled earlier in the production sequence.
SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility assigned to produce the vehicle for this order. Determined during production scheduling.
ISO 8601 production week (e.g., 2025-W23) in which the vehicle is scheduled to be built. Used for production scheduling, capacity planning, and OTD tracking.
The date by which the dealer has requested the vehicle to be delivered to the dealership. Used for On-Time Delivery (OTD) target setting and production scheduling.
OEM sales region code associated with the ordering dealer. Used for regional sales performance reporting, allocation management, and incentive program eligibility.
Externally-known SAP SD sales order number assigned by the OEM ERP system upon order confirmation. Used for cross-system traceability between the lakehouse and SAP S/4HANA.
The logistics mode used to transport the vehicle from the production plant to the dealership: rail, truck (auto carrier), ocean vessel, or a combination.
The channel or system through which the dealer order was submitted to the OEM: dealer portal, CDK Global DMS integration, EDI transmission, manual entry, or API.
The transmission type specified for the ordered vehicle: automatic, manual, Continuously Variable Transmission (CVT), or Dual-Clutch Transmission (DCT).
The timestamp when the dealer order record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks lakehouse.
Unique surrogate identifier for each parts inventory record at the dealer location. Primary key for the dealer parts inventory entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Traceability for recalls and quality requires linking each dealer part stock item to its inbound part record.
Reference to the dealer location where this parts inventory record is maintained. Links to the dealer master entity in the dealer domain.
Reference to the dealer location where this parts inventory record is maintained. Links to the dealer master entity in the dealer domain.

Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Needed for Parts Inventory Management to associate each part stock with its supplying vendor for ordering, pricing, and compliance.

Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Parts pricing, valuation and regulatory compliance reports require linking dealer parts stock to the master SKU definition.

Foreign key linking to asset.spare_parts_catalog. Business justification: Parts inventory must reference the master parts catalog for pricing, compliance, and warranty reporting; this is required for the Parts Pricing & Compliance Report.

Rolling average monthly sales or usage quantity for this part at the dealer location, as calculated and stored by the CDK DMS parts analysis engine. Used for reorder point setting and stocking level optimization. Stored as a DMS-sourced field, not a lakehouse-computed metric.

Physical storage bin, shelf, or rack location code within the dealer parts warehouse or stockroom where this part is stored. Used by parts staff for picking, receiving, and cycle counting. Managed within CDK Global DMS warehouse management.

The refundable deposit amount charged to the customer for the return of the old/used core part. Applicable only when is_core_part is true. Tracked separately in CDK DMS for core deposit liability accounting.

Timestamp when this parts inventory record was first created in the CDK Global DMS parts module. Represents the business record creation event, not the ETL load time. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary price fields (dealer cost price, list price, retail price) for this parts inventory record. Supports multi-currency dealer networks in international markets.

The price at which the dealer procures this part from the OEM or authorized distributor, net of any dealer program discounts. Used for gross margin calculation and parts profitability analysis. Confidential commercial pricing data.

The business date as of which this inventory record reflects the parts quantities and status. Represents the effective date of the CDK DMS data extract loaded into the Silver Layer. Enables point-in-time inventory reporting and trend analysis.

Current lifecycle status of the part in the dealer inventory. Active parts are available for sale and service. Discontinued parts are no longer manufactured. Superseded parts have been replaced by a newer part number. Backordered parts are on order but not yet received. Restricted parts require special authorization. Obsolete parts are no longer supported. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|discontinued|superseded|backordered|restricted|obsolete — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether this part has a core charge associated with it, requiring the customer to return the old/used part (core) for a refund. Common for remanufactured parts such as alternators, starters, and brake calipers. Drives core deposit tracking in CDK DMS.

Indicates whether this part or fluid is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, and disposal procedures under EPA, DOT, and OSHA regulations. Affects shipping documentation and storage bin assignment.

Indicates whether individual units of this part are tracked by unique serial number for warranty, recall, and traceability purposes. Serialized parts (e.g., ECUs, airbag modules) require serial number capture at point of sale and installation.

Date on which the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count was performed for this part at the dealer location. Used to assess inventory accuracy and schedule next count per IATF 16949 inventory control requirements.

Date on which the most recent stock receipt (goods receipt) was posted for this part at the dealer location. Used to assess inventory freshness, identify slow-moving stock, and support cycle count scheduling.

Date on which this part was most recently sold or issued from the dealer's parts inventory, either over the parts counter or via a repair order. Key metric for identifying slow-moving and non-moving (dead stock) inventory.

Timestamp of the most recent update to this parts inventory record in the CDK Global DMS, including quantity adjustments, price updates, bin location changes, or status changes. Used for change detection in incremental Silver Layer loads.
Expected number of calendar days from purchase order placement to receipt of this part at the dealer location. Used to set reorder points and manage customer promise dates for back-ordered parts.

OEM published list price for this part as defined in the official parts price book. Serves as the baseline for retail pricing and customer quotations at the parts counter. Updated periodically via OEM price file updates in CDK DMS.

Cumulative quantity of lost sales recorded for this part when a customer requested the part but it was not available in stock. Tracked in CDK DMS to support stocking level adjustments and identify demand not captured by sales history.

Upper inventory limit for this part at the dealer location. Prevents over-stocking and excess capital tied up in slow-moving parts. Used in conjunction with reorder point for min-max inventory management in CDK DMS.

The vehicle Model Year (MY) range for which this part is applicable, expressed as a single year (e.g., 2022) or a range (e.g., 2019-2024). Used by parts counter staff to verify fitment and avoid incorrect part installation.

Number of months the current on-hand quantity is expected to last based on the trailing average monthly demand. Sourced directly from CDK DMS parts analysis module. Used to identify overstocked and understocked parts for return-to-OEM decisions.

The official OEM-assigned part number as defined in the parts catalog and PLM system. This is the primary catalog identifier used for ordering, supersession tracking, and warranty claims. Sourced from Siemens Teamcenter PLM and SAP MM material master.

High-level classification category of the part used for inventory segmentation, reporting, and stocking strategy. Categories include mechanical (engine, drivetrain, suspension), body (panels, glass, trim), electrical (ECU, sensors, wiring), accessories (OEM-approved add-ons), fluids, and consumables. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical|body|electrical|accessories|fluids|consumables — promote to reference product]

OEM-defined parts group or commodity code that further classifies the part within the parts classification hierarchy. Used for bulk ordering, supplier management, and warranty analysis. Aligns with SAP MM material group.

Net quantity available for immediate sale or service fulfillment, calculated as quantity on hand minus quantity reserved. Represents the unreserved, physically present stock. Stored as a business field from CDK DMS to avoid real-time recalculation in reporting.

Current physical quantity of the part available in the dealer's parts stockroom as recorded in the CDK DMS. Represents the actual counted or system-tracked stock level used for service fulfillment and parts counter sales.
Total quantity of this part currently on open purchase orders submitted to the OEM or aftermarket supplier but not yet received at the dealer. Used for demand planning and avoiding duplicate orders.

Quantity of this part that has been reserved or allocated to open repair orders, customer back-orders, or service appointments but not yet consumed. Reduces available-to-promise quantity for new service requests.

NHTSA or OEM-assigned recall campaign identifier associated with this part when recall_flag is true. Used to link parts inventory to specific recall campaigns for compliance tracking and OEM reimbursement claims.

Indicates whether this part is associated with an active NHTSA safety recall or OEM field action. Recall-flagged parts may have restricted sale or mandatory installation requirements. Supports compliance with NHTSA recall management obligations.

Minimum stock level threshold at which a replenishment order should be triggered for this part. When quantity on hand falls to or below this level, the DMS generates a replenishment recommendation. Set based on historical demand and lead time.
Standard order quantity to be placed when the reorder point is reached. Represents the economic order quantity (EOQ) or OEM-mandated minimum order quantity for this part at this dealer location.

Dealer-set retail selling price for this part at the parts counter or in service repair orders. May differ from OEM list price based on dealer markup strategy, local market conditions, or promotional pricing.
Code of the returnable container holding this inventory.

Dealer-level Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code used within the CDK Global DMS parts module for inventory tracking, bin management, and point-of-sale transactions. May differ from OEM part number when dealer applies local stocking codes.

Required storage condition for this part to maintain quality and safety compliance. Drives bin assignment and warehouse layout decisions. Flammable and hazardous parts require segregated storage per EPA and DOT regulations.

The date on which this part number was officially superseded by the superseding part number as communicated by the OEM. Used to manage transition inventory and inform service advisors of part number changes.

The OEM part number that replaces this part when it has been superseded. Populated when inventory_status is 'superseded'. Enables the parts counter to automatically redirect orders to the current valid part number. Part of the supersession chain managed in the OEM parts catalog.

The part number assigned by the OEM distributor or aftermarket supplier for cross-reference purposes. Used when ordering from non-OEM sources or when the dealer stocks approved aftermarket alternatives alongside OEM parts.

Standard unit of measure in which the part is stocked, ordered, and sold. EA (each) is most common for discrete parts; KG/L for fluids and chemicals; SET/PAIR for grouped components. Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|SET|BOX|PAIR|ROLL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Comma-separated list of vehicle model names or platform codes for which this part is applicable (e.g., F-150, Explorer, Mustang). Supports fitment verification at the parts counter and in service repair order creation.

Indicates whether this part is eligible for OEM warranty claim submission when installed during a warranty repair. Non-warranty-eligible parts (e.g., customer-pay accessories) are excluded from warranty billing to the OEM.
Unique identifier for the recall completion record. Primary key for the recall completion transaction.
Identifier of the customer who owns the vehicle at the time of recall completion.
Identifier of the dealer location that performed the recall remediation work.
Identifier of the dealer location that performed the recall remediation work.
Identifier of the customer who owns the vehicle at the time of recall completion.
Identifier of the repair order under which the recall work was performed.
Employee identifier of the technician who performed the recall remediation work. Used for quality tracking and reimbursement validation.
Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Recall completion tracking must reference the VIN registry to ensure the correct vehicle is marked as completed.
Classification of the recall or campaign. Safety recalls are NHTSA-mandated, service campaigns are voluntary OEM actions, TSB (Technical Service Bulletin) are informational fixes.
Date when the recall remediation work was completed and the vehicle was returned to the customer. Used for NHTSA compliance reporting and OEM recall closure tracking.
Business identifier for the recall completion transaction, typically generated by the DMS or OEM recall management system.
Current status of the recall completion work. Completed indicates full remediation; incomplete or parts_pending indicate work not yet finished.
Precise date and time when the recall work was marked complete in the DMS system.
Timestamp when this recall completion record was first created in the data system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD).
Date when the customer was notified that the recall work was completed. Used for customer satisfaction tracking and communication audit.
Method used to notify the customer of recall completion: email, SMS, phone call, postal mail, or in-person at dealer.
Unique transaction identifier from the CDK Global DMS system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation between DMS and OEM systems.
Whether the recall was completed via home/mobile service.
Total labor cost for the recall work, calculated based on labor hours and dealer labor rate. Used for OEM reimbursement to dealer.
Total labor hours spent on the recall remediation work. Used for dealer reimbursement calculation and efficiency analysis.
Specific model designation of the vehicle that received recall work.
Model year of the vehicle that received the recall work. Used for recall eligibility verification and reporting.
Brand or nameplate of the vehicle (e.g., F-150, Camry, Silverado). Used to segment recall completion rates by product line.

business_glossary_term: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) Report Date
description: Date when the recall completion was reported to NHTSA. Required for compliance tracking and audit trails.

business_glossary_term: National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) Report Flag
description: Indicates whether this recall completion has been reported to NHTSA as required for safety recalls. True means reported; false means not yet reported or not required.
Vehicle odometer reading at the time of recall completion, typically in kilometers or miles. Used for warranty validation and vehicle history tracking.
Unit of measure for the odometer reading: km (kilometers) or mi (miles).
Total cost of parts used in the recall remediation. OEM reimburses dealer for parts at wholesale cost.
Comma-separated or pipe-separated list of quantities corresponding to parts_used_list. Used for parts consumption analysis.
Comma-separated or pipe-separated list of part numbers used in the recall remediation. Used for parts consumption tracking and inventory replenishment.
Technician notes from the post-repair inspection confirming that the recall remediation was successful and the vehicle is safe to operate.

Type of powertrain: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Some recalls are powertrain-specific.
Technician notes from the pre-repair inspection documenting the condition of the vehicle and the recall-related issue before remediation.
Date when the quality verification or audit of the recall work was performed.
Indicates whether the recall work was subject to quality verification or audit by the dealer service manager or OEM field representative. True means verified; false means not verified.
Name of the person who performed the quality verification or audit of the recall work (typically service manager or OEM field representative).
Official recall or service campaign number issued by NHTSA or the OEM. Used to track which specific safety recall or service action was completed.
Claim number submitted by the dealer to the OEM for reimbursement of recall work. Links to the OEM financial system for payment processing.
Date when the OEM paid the dealer for the recall work. Used for dealer cash flow analysis and OEM financial reporting.
Status of the dealer reimbursement claim. Pending indicates claim submitted but not yet reviewed; approved means claim validated; paid means funds transferred to dealer.
Detailed description of the remediation work performed to address the recall. Includes parts replaced, adjustments made, software updates applied, etc.
Cost of any sublet or outsourced work required to complete the recall (e.g., specialized welding, paint work). Reimbursable by OEM if pre-approved.
Certification or license number of the technician. Some recalls require certified technicians to perform the work.
Full name of the technician who completed the recall work. Used for performance tracking and quality audits.
Total amount claimed by the dealer for reimbursement from the OEM, including labor, parts, and sublet costs.
Timestamp when this recall completion record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
Unique identifier for the service capacity configuration record. Primary key.
Reference to the dealer location that owns this service capacity configuration.
Reference to the dealer location that owns this service capacity configuration.
Number of A-level (master) technicians certified for complex diagnostics, ECU (Electronic Control Unit) programming, and advanced repair work.

business_glossary_term: Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) Certified Technician Count
description: Number of technicians certified for ADAS calibration, sensor diagnostics, and autonomous driving system service.

business_glossary_term: Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) Service Bays
description: Number of service bays equipped with ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) calibration equipment for sensor alignment and diagnostic work.

Total productive labor hours available per day across all technicians, calculated as (technician headcount × hours per shift × efficiency factor). Used for service appointment scheduling and capacity utilization analysis.

business_glossary_term: Average Repair Order (RO) Cycle Time Hours
description: Average time in hours from RO (Repair Order) open to close. Used for scheduling optimization and capacity planning.
Number of B-level (journeyman) technicians certified for general maintenance and standard repair operations.
Number of service bays designated for collision repair, body work, and paint operations.
Number of technicians certified for collision repair, body work, and paint operations.
Number of C-level (apprentice) technicians performing basic maintenance tasks and assisting senior technicians.
Business identifier for the service capacity configuration, typically used in DMS (Dealer Management System) integration and capacity planning reports.
Free-text notes regarding capacity constraints, planned expansions, seasonal adjustments, or special considerations for service scheduling.

Current operational status of the service capacity configuration. Active indicates normal operations, seasonal indicates temporary capacity adjustments, reduced/expanded indicate capacity changes, under_review indicates pending capacity assessment.
Timestamp when this service capacity configuration record was first created in the system.
Current utilization rate of service capacity expressed as a percentage. Calculated as (actual labor hours used / available labor hours) × 100. Used for capacity planning and scheduling optimization.
Percentage of total service capacity allocated for customer pay (non-warranty) repair orders. Typically 60-70% of total capacity.

Classification of diagnostic equipment capability. Basic: OBD scanners. Standard: factory scan tools. Advanced: ECU programming and ADAS calibration. Premium: full factory diagnostic suite with remote technical assistance.

business_glossary_term: Dealer Management System (DMS) Capacity Record ID
description: External identifier for this capacity configuration in the source DMS system. Used for data lineage and integration reconciliation.
Code identifying the DMS platform used by the dealer for service scheduling and capacity management. Primary integration point for capacity data synchronization.
Date when this service capacity configuration expires or is superseded. Nullable for open-ended configurations.
Date when this service capacity configuration becomes effective. Used for capacity planning and scheduling optimization.
Number of technicians holding OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) certification for EV, HEV, and PHEV high-voltage system service and repair.
Number of service bays equipped and certified for EV (Electric Vehicle), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), and PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle) service work.
Number of service bays designated for quick service operations such as oil changes, tire rotations, and PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection).
Number of service bays designated for general maintenance and repair work on ICE (Internal Combustion Engine) vehicles.
Indicates whether the dealer offers mobile service capability for on-site customer vehicle service and OTA updates.
Standard duration of each work shift in hours, typically 8.0 or 10.0 hours.
Percentage of total service capacity allocated for internal work including PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection), loaner maintenance, and dealer-owned vehicle service. Typically 10-15% of total capacity.
Date of the most recent capacity assessment and configuration review. Used for capacity planning governance and audit trails.
Total number of loaner vehicles available for customer use during service appointments. Used for customer satisfaction and service capacity planning.
Current utilization rate of the loaner fleet expressed as a percentage. Calculated as (loaners in use / total loaner fleet size) × 100.
Maximum number of service appointments that can be scheduled per day based on bay capacity, technician availability, and average repair order duration.
Number of available mobile/home service slots per day.
Number of technicians dedicated to mobile service operations. Null if mobile service is not offered.
Scheduled date for the next capacity assessment and configuration review. Typically quarterly or semi-annually.
Number of days per week the service department operates. Typically 5 or 6 days for most dealers.
Total square footage of parts storage capacity at the dealer service location. Used for parts inventory planning and JIT (Just-in-Time) delivery optimization.
Number of work shifts operated per day (1 for single shift, 2 for double shift operations).

Comma-separated list of specialty certifications held by the service department, such as EV, ADAS, body shop, OBD (On-Board Diagnostics), TPMS (Tire Pressure Monitoring System), OTA (Over-the-Air Update) programming. Used for service capability assessment and customer routing.
Target utilization rate for optimal service department performance, typically 80-85%. Used for capacity planning and performance scorecards.
Total number of physical service bays available at the dealer service department for vehicle maintenance and repair work.
Total number of certified technicians employed at the dealer service department across all skill levels.
Timestamp when this service capacity configuration record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trails.
Percentage of total service capacity allocated for warranty and recall work. Typically 20-30% of total capacity.
Unique surrogate identifier for each vehicle allocation record linking a specific vehicle unit or allocation batch from OEM manufacturing output to a dealership inventory pipeline.
Reference to the manufacturing production order that produced the allocated vehicle units. Links manufacturing output to dealer allocation pipeline.
Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Allocation planning reports require linking each allocated vehicle to its SKU for capacity, cost, and compliance tracking.
Reference to the dealership receiving this vehicle allocation. Links to the dealer master record in the dealer network.
Reference to the dealership receiving this vehicle allocation. Links to the dealer master record in the dealer network.
Date by which the dealer must formally accept or reject this allocation. Failure to respond by this date may result in automatic acceptance or reallocation per OEM dealer agreement terms.
Exact timestamp when the dealer formally accepted this vehicle allocation via the dealer portal or DMS integration. Provides precise audit trail for dealer commitment.
Number of vehicle units formally accepted by the dealer from this allocation. May be less than allocated_quantity if the dealer partially accepts or rejects units.
Actual date the allocated vehicle(s) were physically delivered to and received by the dealership. Used for On-Time Delivery (OTD) performance measurement and dealer satisfaction tracking.
Identifier grouping multiple allocation records issued in the same OEM allocation run or production release cycle. Enables batch-level tracking, reporting, and dealer communication.
The business event date on which the OEM formally allocated the vehicle unit(s) to the dealership. Represents the principal real-world event time for this transaction.
Externally-known business identifier for this allocation transaction, used in dealer communications, DMS integration, and OEM-dealer correspondence. Format: ALLOC-{MY}-{sequence}.

Code identifying the specific allocation rule or algorithm applied to determine this dealer's vehicle entitlement (e.g., turn-and-earn, market share, historical sales, regional quota). Drives transparency and auditability of allocation decisions.
Current lifecycle state of the vehicle allocation record. Tracks progression from OEM-initiated pending through dealer acceptance or rejection to final delivery confirmation.

Classification of the allocation purpose: standard retail inventory, priority customer order fulfillment, constrained supply allocation, fleet customer, dealer demonstrator vehicle, or loaner vehicle program.
Timestamp when this allocation record was first captured in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this allocation record (MSRP, dealer invoice price, incentive amounts). Supports multi-currency dealer networks in international markets.

The price at which the OEM invoices the dealer for the allocated vehicle, net of holdback and before dealer incentives. Represents the dealer's cost basis for inventory financing and margin calculation.

The corresponding allocation or stock order reference number in the dealer's CDK Global Dealer Management System (DMS). Enables end-to-end reconciliation between OEM allocation records and dealer inventory systems.

Estimated date the allocated vehicle(s) are expected to arrive at the dealership. Calculated based on production schedule, transport lead time, and PDI processing time. Communicated to dealers for inventory planning.

Code indicating any active hold placed on the allocated vehicle(s) preventing retail sale (e.g., safety recall hold, quality hold, regulatory compliance hold, stop-sale order). Null when no hold is active. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: safety_recall|quality_hold|regulatory|stop_sale|tsb_hold|other — promote to reference product]
Monetary value of the dealer incentive or bonus applicable to this specific allocation record. Represents the financial benefit to the dealer for accepting and retailing this vehicle.

Code identifying the OEM dealer incentive program applicable to this allocation (e.g., volume bonus, stair-step program, conquest incentive). Links to the dealer incentive program master for payout calculation.

Indicates whether this allocation is fulfilling a specific retail customer order (sold order) rather than being allocated to dealer stock inventory. Drives order fulfillment priority and customer communication workflows.
Preferred last-mile delivery method for this allocation (truck, rail, self-drive).

Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for the allocated vehicle unit in the base transaction currency. Used for dealer invoice calculation, incentive program application, and consumer pricing guidance.
Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or dealer-specific comments associated with this allocation record. Used by zone managers and dealer relations teams.
Indicates whether the Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) has been completed for the allocated vehicle(s) at the dealership. Required before vehicle can be retailed to a customer.
Indicates whether a Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) is required for the allocated vehicle(s) before retail handover. Drives dealer service scheduling and PDI cost recovery processes.

Code identifying the port or processing compound through which imported or CKD/SKD vehicles pass before onward delivery to the dealer. Relevant for international allocation flows and customs compliance.

Dealer priority tier assigned for this allocation cycle, determining precedence when vehicle supply is constrained. Tier 1 dealers receive first allocation rights based on performance metrics, sales volume, or strategic importance.
SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the allocated vehicle(s) were or will be produced. Used for logistics routing, transport cost calculation, and supply chain traceability.
OEM regional zone code (e.g., NORTHEAST, SOUTHEAST, CENTRAL, WEST) grouping territories for macro-level allocation planning, regional incentive programs, and zone manager oversight.

Standardized code indicating the dealer's reason for rejecting this allocation (e.g., excess inventory, wrong specification, cash flow constraints, market demand mismatch). Null when allocation is accepted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: excess_inventory|wrong_spec|cash_flow|demand_mismatch|facility_capacity|other — promote to reference product]
Planned date on which the allocated vehicle unit(s) are scheduled to enter the production line. Derived from the Start of Production (SOP) schedule and used for delivery window planning.

Identifies the operational system of record that originated this allocation record. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration auditing across SAP S/4HANA SD, CDK Global DMS, and Salesforce Automotive Cloud.
OEM-defined sales territory code within which the dealer operates. Used for regional allocation quota management, zone-level supply balancing, and territory performance reporting.

Primary mode of transportation used to deliver the allocated vehicle(s) from the production plant or port of entry to the dealership. Drives logistics cost allocation and delivery lead time estimation.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this allocation record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.

17-character Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) assigned to a specific vehicle unit within this allocation. Populated for unit-level allocations; null for batch-level allocations where VINs are not yet assigned.
Unique surrogate key for the connected vehicle record.
Identifier of the fleet to which the vehicle belongs, if applicable.
Identifier of the fleet to which the vehicle belongs, if applicable.

Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Sales Attribution Report links each connected vehicle to the dealer that sold it, enabling dealer‑level warranty and service responsibility.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Fleet driver assignment report requires linking each vehicle to its current driver employee for compliance and usage tracking.

Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Vehicle Asset Management report requires linking each connected vehicle to its equipment registry record for warranty, maintenance, and depreciation tracking.
Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Regulatory Homologation Report requires linking each vehicle to its homologation record for market approval tracking.

Foreign key linking to aftersales.nameplate. Business justification: OTA campaign planning requires linking each connected vehicle to its product nameplate to apply correct software version and compliance reporting.
Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Needed to identify the legal owner for billing, data‑privacy compliance, and service entitlement of each connected vehicle.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Each vehicle contributes to a profit center for margin analysis; required for the Vehicle Profitability Report.

Foreign key linking to mobility.software_version. Business justification: Storing the software version as a FK enables consistent version management and eliminates duplicated version strings on the vehicle record.
Unique identifier of the telematics hardware installed in the vehicle.
Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Required for warranty, recall and OTA management linking each connected vehicle to the official VIN registry record.
Current lifecycle state of the device activation.
Date and time when the device was first activated.
Estimated health of the battery relative to its original capacity.
Current state of charge of the vehicle's battery expressed as a percentage.
Service tier defining data allowance and priority for the vehicle.
Timestamp when the connected vehicle record was first created in the lakehouse.
Subscription plan governing cellular data usage.
Cumulative cellular data consumed by the vehicle in the current billing cycle.
Date when the data usage counter was last reset.
Date and time when the device was deactivated or retired.
Model or family of the connected telematics device.
Overall health status derived from the latest diagnostic data.
Current firmware version installed on the telematics device.
Three‑letter ISO country code representing the primary market of the vehicle.
Timestamp of the most recent vehicle diagnostic event.
Most recent Diagnostic Trouble Code reported by the vehicle.
Timestamp of the most recent Over‑The‑Air software update.
Timestamp of the most recent TPMS firmware or configuration update.
Timestamp of the most recent V2X software update.
Original Equipment Manufacturer of the vehicle.
Total distance traveled by the vehicle, reported in kilometers.
Timestamp of the most recent mileage reading.
Marketing name of the vehicle model.
Model year of the vehicle (calendar year).
Indicates whether the vehicle supports Over-The-Air updates.
Primary propulsion technology of the vehicle.
Date the vehicle was enrolled in the connected mobility program.
Current status of the vehicle's connectivity service registration.
Integrated Circuit Card Identifier of the SIM/eSIM used for connectivity.
International Mobile Subscriber Identity associated with the SIM.
Version of the vehicle's on‑board software platform.
Date when the current subscription plan expires (null if open‑ended).
Service plan governing feature entitlements for the connected vehicle.
Date when the current subscription plan became effective.
Indicates whether the vehicle is equipped with Tire Pressure Monitoring System telemetry.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the connected vehicle record.
Indicates whether the vehicle can communicate Vehicle‑to‑Everything.
Broad category of the vehicle.
Globally unique identifier assigned to each vehicle by the manufacturer.
Date when the vehicle's warranty coverage ends.
Current warranty coverage status of the vehicle.
Unique system-generated identifier for the geofence.
Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Geofence speed limits and restrictions depend on jurisdictional regulations; linking enables jurisdiction‑specific enforcement.
Identifier of the fleet account to which the geofence belongs.
Current activation state of the geofence.
Vehicle categories permitted to enter the geofence.
Calculated surface area of the geofence.
Name of the mobility service (e.g., OTA, V2X) that uses this geofence.
Result of the latest audit (passed, failed, or pending).
Latitude of the geofence centre point (used for circular geofences).
Longitude of the geofence centre point (used for circular geofences).
City where the geofence is located.
Regulatory compliance state of the geofence definition.
Country of the geofence location.
Timestamp when the geofence record was first created.
Number of days telemetry related to this geofence is retained.
Date when the geofence expires or is de‑commissioned (nullable).
Date when the geofence becomes effective.
Identifier used by external systems to reference this geofence.
Business-visible code used to reference the geofence in external systems.
Detailed description of the geofence purpose and characteristics.
Human‑readable name of the geofence.
Well‑Known Text (WKT) representation of the geofence geometry.
Shape type that defines the geofence geometry.
Indicates whether this geofence is the default for its purpose.
Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit for the geofence.
Maximum vehicle speed allowed within the geofence.
Minimum vehicle speed required within the geofence.
Free‑form field for any supplemental information.
Maximum cumulative odometer reading allowed while inside the geofence.
Total perimeter length of the geofence boundary.
Business purpose of the geofence, e.g., fleet zone, ODD, restricted area.
Radius of the geofence when geometry_type is circle.
Three‑letter ISO code representing the region of the geofence.
State or province of the geofence location.
User identifier that performed the latest update.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the geofence record.
Incremental version of the geofence definition for change tracking.
User identifier that created the geofence record.
System-generated unique identifier for each geofence event record.
Unique identifier of the defined geofence area.
Identifier of the telematics hardware that reported the geofence event.
Remaining battery charge of the vehicle (relevant for EV/HEV) at the event time.
Timestamp when the geofence event record was first created in the lakehouse.
Indicates whether the event data passed validation checks (true = valid).
Length of time the vehicle remained inside the geofence before exiting; populated for exit events.
Communication channel used to transmit the geofence event data.
Date and time when the vehicle crossed the geofence boundary.
Type of geofence interaction: entry, exit, or dwell within the boundary.
Geographic latitude of the vehicle at the geofence crossing point.
Geographic longitude of the vehicle at the geofence crossing point.
Vehicle odometer reading at the time of the event, expressed in kilometers.
Current processing state of the event within the data pipeline.
Radio signal strength of the telematics device when the event was recorded.
Speed of the vehicle at the moment of crossing, measured in kilometers per hour.
Business action automatically initiated by the event, such as sending an alert, activating a service, or notifying fleet management.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the geofence event record.
Globally unique identifier of the vehicle involved in the geofence event.
Unique identifier for the mobility_dtc_event data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).
Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Add direct link to connected_vehicle for DTC events to enable vehicle‑level diagnostics.
Timestamp when DTC was cleared
Diagnostic trouble code (OBD-II format)
Timestamp when DTC was triggered
Freeze frame data captured at time of DTC
Whether DTC is currently active
Vehicle mileage when DTC occurred
Severity level (informational, warning, critical)
Vehicle system affected (engine, transmission, brakes, ADAS)
System‑generated unique identifier for each predictive maintenance alert record.
Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Link vehicle VIN to master connected_vehicle record; VIN column redundant.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Maintenance alerts trigger cost allocations to the plant's cost center; required for the Predictive Maintenance Cost Forecast report.
High‑level classification of the alert (e.g., engine, brake, battery, software).
Business identifier code assigned to the alert for tracking and reference.
Current lifecycle state of the alert.
Name of the vehicle component or system predicted to fail (e.g., engine, brake, battery).
Model confidence that the predicted failure will occur, expressed as a percentage (0‑100).
Timestamp when the alert record was first persisted in the lakehouse.
Textual description of the failure mode the model predicts (e.g., coolant leak, battery degradation).
Timestamp when the predictive maintenance alert was generated by the analytics engine.
Odometer reading of the vehicle at the time the alert was generated.
Latest timestamp when the predicted failure is expected to occur.
Earliest timestamp when the predicted failure is expected to occur.
Prescribed maintenance or repair action to address the predicted issue.
Timestamp when the alert was marked resolved or closed.
Severity classification of the alert indicating potential impact.
Ambient temperature recorded by the vehicle sensor when the alert was generated.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the alert record.
System-generated unique identifier for the remote command record.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Remote command services incur operational costs tracked against a cost center; needed for the Remote Command Cost Allocation analysis.
Identifier of the customer who initiated the command (if applicable).
Identifier of the fleet operator issuing the command (if applicable).
Identifier of the fleet operator issuing the command (if applicable).
Identifier of the customer who initiated the command (if applicable).
Date‑time when the vehicle acknowledged receipt of the command.
Serialized JSON string containing command‑specific parameters (e.g., target temperature, charge schedule details).
Business identifier or reference code for the remote command issued.
Origin of the command request (customer mobile app, fleet management portal, automated rule engine, or call center).
Category of command sent to the vehicle (e.g., remote start, lock/unlock, climate pre‑conditioning, horn/lights, charge scheduling, speed limit set).
Timestamp when the remote command record was first created in the data lake.
Date‑time when the command was successfully delivered to the vehicle's telematics unit.
Result of the command execution on the vehicle.
Time after which the command is no longer valid if not executed.
Textual description of why the command failed, if execution_status is failed.
Date‑time when the remote command was created and sent to the vehicle.
Priority level assigned to the command for processing.
Optional future time at which the command should be executed.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the remote command record.
Unique vehicle identifier to which the command was sent.
System-generated unique identifier for the remote diagnostic session.
Foreign key linking to mobility.connected_vehicle. Business justification: Remote diagnostic sessions are performed per vehicle; link to connected_vehicle for context.
Internal identifier of the driver or vehicle owner associated with the session.
Internal identifier of the driver or vehicle owner associated with the session.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Remote diagnostic audit logs must record the technician employee performing the session for quality and liability tracking.
Battery charge level reported by the vehicle during the session, expressed as a percentage.
Current network connectivity state of the vehicle during the session.
Timestamp when the session record was first created in the lakehouse.
Amount of telemetry and diagnostic data transferred during the session.
Scope of the remote diagnostic session: full vehicle scan, targeted ECU, or custom set of modules.
Comma‑separated list of error codes returned by the vehicle during diagnostics.
Indicates whether the session was escalated to after‑sales service for further action.
Version identifier of the vehicle ECU firmware queried in the session.
Communication medium used for the remote session.
Free‑form technician or system notes captured for the session.
Result of the diagnostic session after processing.
Comma‑separated list of action codes suggested by the diagnostic engine (e.g., repair steps, software patches).
Current processing state of the remote diagnostic session.
External reference code for the diagnostic session, used in service logs and customer communications.
Total elapsed time of the remote diagnostic session in seconds.
Exact time the remote diagnostic session was started.
Measured radio signal strength in decibel‑milliwatts at session start.
Code of the event that initiated the session (e.g., DTC code).
Origin of the session trigger – Diagnostic Trouble Code event, manual request, scheduled maintenance, or OTA update.
Identifiers of TSBs surfaced during the session, if any.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to the session record.
Odometer reading of the vehicle at the time of the session, in kilometers.
Unique surrogate key for each software version record.
Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT: Software licensing and regulatory reporting need the software vendor (supplier) identified for each version.
Cryptographic hash of the software file used for integrity verification.
Algorithm used to generate the checksum value.
Indicates whether the checksum has been successfully validated after upload.
Comma‑separated list of vehicle model identifiers that can receive this software version.
Name of the software component or ECU that the version applies to (e.g., Infotainment ECU, ADAS Controller).
Timestamp when the software version record was first created in the data lake.
Secure URL from which the software package can be retrieved for OTA deployment.
Container or encoding format of the software package.
Internal storage path or bucket location where the software binary resides.
Size of the software binary or package in bytes.
Textual description of hardware prerequisites beyond the minimum version (e.g., specific sensor revisions).
True if the update must be applied to maintain compliance or safety.
Indicates whether the release contains security‑related patches.
Lowest hardware/ECU version required for this software to operate.
Date on which regulatory approval was granted.
Reference to the regulatory approval document (e.g., UNECE WP.29 R156 SUMS).
Current status of the regulatory approval for this software version.
Planned cadence for releases of this software component.
Calendar date on which the software version was officially released.
Detailed release notes documenting bug fixes, new features, and known issues.
Classification of the release indicating its purpose and maturity.
Internal code used by engineering to track the release (e.g., RV20230401).
Free‑text description of the software version, its purpose and key changes.
Lifecycle status of the software version within the OTA ecosystem.
Powertrain categories for which the software version is applicable.
Identifier of the electronic control unit (ECU) or system targeted by this software version.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the software version record.
Human‑readable version identifier following the vendor's versioning scheme (e.g., 2023.04.01).
Unique surrogate key for each telematics control unit (TCU) record.

Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Device Installation Management process tracks which equipment asset each telematics device is mounted on for service contracts and calibration compliance.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Device installation/maintenance logs need the installer employee to support warranty validation and service cost allocation.
Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Supports hardware warranty and recall process linking each telematics device to its manufacturing supplier for defect tracking.
Current battery charge level expressed as a percentage.
Indicates whether the device has passed factory calibration (true) or not (false).
Timestamp when the telematics device record was first created in the data lake.
Date when the current data plan expires.
Type of cellular data plan assigned to the device.
Name of the manufacturer that produced the telematics device.
Model designation of the telematics device as defined by the manufacturer.
Overall lifecycle status of the device.
Current firmware version installed on the telematics device.
Most recent latitude coordinate reported by the device.
Most recent longitude coordinate reported by the device.
Generation identifier for the hardware platform of the device (e.g., Gen1, Gen2).
Unique identifier of the SIM card embedded in the telematics device.
Globally unique identifier for the device's cellular modem.
Date the telematics device was installed in the vehicle.
When the device last received a firmware OTA update.
Timestamp of the most recent health‑check ping received from the device.
Date of the most recent scheduled maintenance performed on the device.
Planned interval in months between routine maintenance events.
Cumulative distance traveled as reported by the device, in kilometers.
Current operational state of the device within the fleet.
Indicates whether the device supports over‑the‑air firmware updates.
Entity type that owns the device.
Date the device was provisioned and linked to the fleet management system.
Cellular signal strength measured in decibel‑milliwatts.
Internal temperature of the telematics unit in degrees Celsius.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the telematics device record.
Flag indicating if vehicle‑to‑everything communication is enabled on the device.
VIN of the vehicle in which the device is installed.
Date when the device's manufacturer warranty ends.
Unique surrogate key for each telemetry event record.

Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_registry. Business justification: Telemetry Data Integration report joins raw telemetry events with equipment assets for reliability analysis and warranty claim validation.
Unique identifier of the driver associated with the vehicle at event time.
Identifier linking the telemetry event to a specific vehicle trip or route.

Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Allows raw telemetry events to be associated with the official vehicle record for diagnostics, safety reporting and emissions compliance.
Elevation above sea level of the vehicle at event time, in meters.
Current electric battery charge level for EV/HEV vehicles, expressed as a percentage.
Instantaneous voltage of the vehicle's high‑voltage battery, expressed in volts.
True if the vehicle is currently connected to a charger and charging, otherwise false.
True if the telematics device had an active network connection at event time.
Engine speed measured in revolutions per minute at the time of the event.
Measured temperature of the engine coolant at event time, in degrees Celsius.
Monotonically increasing sequence number for events from the same vehicle, used for ordering.
Identifier of the source system that produced the telemetry record (e.g., Geotab, Bosch).
Date and time when the telemetry event was generated by the vehicle sensor.
Code that categorizes the type of telemetry event (e.g., speed, harsh_brake, idle).
Version identifier of the telematics device firmware that generated the event.
Remaining fuel level expressed as a percentage of tank capacity.
Estimated horizontal accuracy of the GPS position in meters.
Compass direction the vehicle is facing at event time, expressed in degrees from true north.
Indicates whether the vehicle ignition is on (true) or off (false) at the moment of the event.
Geographic latitude of the vehicle at event time, expressed in decimal degrees.
Estimated vertical accuracy of the latitude measurement.
City name derived from the GPS coordinates at event time.
Three‑letter ISO country code derived from the GPS coordinates at event time.
State or province name derived from the GPS coordinates at event time.
Geographic longitude of the vehicle at event time, expressed in decimal degrees.
Estimated vertical accuracy of the longitude measurement.
Cumulative distance traveled by the vehicle, recorded in kilometers.
Original JSON payload received from the telematics device before any transformation.
Timestamp when the telemetry record was first ingested into the silver layer.
